data.narrative:
Apex Space, a Series D space and aerospace company founded by Ian Cinnamon and Max Benassi, has raised $500M+ toward a stated $1B+ valuation. The target sits within a universe of eight private peers at similar or adjacent stages, alongside five public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the last post-money round of $1.0B, implying $1.5B. Peer-median methodology across eight same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields $1.6B, with the peer set ranging from $1.0B to $3.5B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied; the target's revenue is not on file, which is typical for private companies. The median public Space & Aerospace P/S is 53.2x across six tickers, but is not usable without revenue disclosure.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a range of $1.3B (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.6B. This implies modest upside from the stated $1.0B last round. The valuation remains sensitive to revenue trajectory and capital efficiency—metrics not provided here. Public market comparables suggest potential for substantially higher valuations upon revenue scale and exit, but current private-market evidence supports the $1.3B–$1.9B range.
